title: chromolaena iridolepis
intro: Chromolaena iridolepis, also known as Mexican mint or tree marigold, is a plant species in the sunflower family that is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, respiratory problems, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Chromolaena iridolepis can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in natural remedies and traditional medicine.
